scope_name: durable evidence rescue and benchmark-input lock
purpose: Rescue N2-C's and N2-A.1's real acquisition/capture evidence out of expiring GitHub Actions artifacts into durable, independently-verifiable storage, before any N2-D arm-identity/tokenizer/determinism/benchmark work begins. N2-D0 performs no comparison, no execution, and no measurement of QODEC or RTK.

frozen_boundary.rule: N2-C is immutable. PR #54, every N2-C file, the N2-C workflow, the committed N2-C registry, and qodec/evals/interop/v2/n2/source-freeze/** must not be modified by N2-D0. lock_durable_sources.py must not be run against its default destination (it writes into the frozen N2-C tree and mutates the N2-C registry); its verification/content-addressing logic may be read-only reused or adapted, but every new file and identity created here belongs to N2-D0.

durable_storage_decision.mechanism: GitHub Release + tag, dedicated to N2-D0
rejected_mechanisms: committing raw acquisition archives into ordinary git history
committed_to_git: release/tag and asset identities (recorded inside the manifest)
durable_storage_decision.stored_as_release_assets: the actual acquisition/capture archives (original GitHub Actions artifact ZIPs, re-uploaded byte-identical)
durable_storage_decision.independent_verifiability_requirement: the committed manifest must let anyone verify durable storage using only public, anonymous HTTP requests to the release-asset download URLs plus the hashes already committed in this manifest — even if a release asset were later replaced or deleted, the manifest alone proves what SHOULD be there and lets drift be detected.

required_artifacts: 37 acquisition-* artifacts (one per eligible N2-C candidate, 17 primary + 20 alternate)

n2a_evidence_to_preserve.identification_rule: identified from PR #52's own accepted evidence trail (the three verification runs listed on the final head), not inferred from the latest or an investigation-only run

canonical_input_rule.rule: Unless the accepted evidence dictates otherwise, capture-a is the canonical benchmark input; capture-b is retained solely as reproducibility evidence. Never choose between captures based on later QODEC or RTK results.

n2a_evidence_to_preserve.if_missing: stop and report the gap; never silently regenerate the capture and call it the original evidence

verification_chain: 1. workflow run ID, head SHA, workflow identity, and successful conclusion
3. downloaded artifact archive digest against GitHub artifact metadata and the accepted artifact index
5. each acquisition receipt and normalized archive against the folded identities in the accepted n2-source-freeze and source-identity-reports artifacts
6. candidate count and role mapping against the accepted final selection report
7. no missing, duplicate, unexpected, or extra candidate artifacts
archive-safety validation (archive_security.assert_safe, read-only-reused from N2-C) is performed before extracting any downloaded artifact
explicit_disclosure: The checked-in N2-C candidate-registry.json and source-manifests intentionally leave real-acquisition identity fields null pre-lock (per N2-C's own documented two-phase identity-lock contract). The folded, real values exist only in the accepted n2-source-freeze and source-identity-reports workflow artifacts from run 29404265568 — that is what this verification chain actually checks against, never the committed registry's null placeholders.
publication_proof_required: download every asset again through the public release-asset path without a repository checkout or local build cache
re-verify the complete contained-file hash map
reconstruct the 18-case N2 primary input set (N2-A canary + 17 N2-C primary cases) and the 20-case alternate order
prove the reconstructed set matches the accepted N2-C selection and the accepted N2-A canary identity
a release upload succeeding is NOT itself acceptance evidence -- only the post-publication download and re-verification is
scope_boundary_prohibited_actions: declare or imply a benchmark winner
stop_after_reporting: the complete artifact inventory and total sizes
the implementation checkpoint, if all required bytes are still available

carried_forward_findings_for_the_later_n.qodec_identity: must include both the source-boundary commit touching qodec/src/** and the full built-binary SHA256 (qodec has no --version flag; identity is sha256(binary), not a git tag)
carried_forward_findings_for_the_later_n.rtk_identity: tools.lock.toml pins rtk v0.42.4 (cargo install --git https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk --tag v0.42.4, sha256 61683c0a25fb58e2e8e7d5a8067c6111956be75225daa2edfbc389a066f7133f), but qodec/evals/interop/v2/pilot/pilot-manifest.json records a DIFFERENT observed RTK build identity (short-hash 5d32d073) exhibiting real, observed nondeterminism on --filter log. These two identities must be reconciled before any N2-D canary is run.
carried_forward_findings_for_the_later_n.tokenizer_identity: must pin the exact o200k_base implementation/dependency lock and test vectors, not merely the string "o200k_base" -- a real, in-repo candidate already exists at qodec/src/meter.rs (Bpe wrapping tiktoken_rs::o200k_base()).
carried_forward_findings_for_the_later_n.n1_continuity_set: qodec/evals/interop/v2/pilot/ (10 real cases, pilot-manifest.json) -- NOT qodec/evals/interop/v2/corpus/**, which is an unrelated, empty (benchmark_case_count: 0) corpus-compiler scaffold.
